content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660              322-5901           322-6441                327-6444

July 1, 1981


Assemblyman Howard Berman
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Assemblyman Berman:

Thank you for your letter of June 30, 1981, in which you
ask us to comment on a going away party for your aide, Jack
Mayesh.

First, we must emphasize that the total value of the gift
(the rental of the hall, food, drink and any other gifts) pro-
vided to any legislator, legislative official or administrative
official may not exceed $10 per person since lobbyists under
Government Code Section 86203 may not make gifts totaling more
than $10 per person in a calendar month. Further, the entire
gift is charged to each lobbyist who contributed, even if the
lobbyist only provides, for example $3 per person of the expen-
ses. Finally, each lobbyist must ensure that this function and
other functions do not combine to exceed $10 in the calendar
month for any person named above.

Each lobbyist must take due care to ensure that the lobby-
ist lists names of those persons who attend. The method you
have outlined appears to satisfy this requirement. A guest
book will be at the front door and will be "manned" between
5:30 and 6:30. After 6:30 you have suggested that a sign be
placed above the book asking each guest to sign. However, we
believe it is preferable that a person be with the guest book
at all times so that each attendee is asked to register. After
the event, you and your staff should review the guest book and
add names of those persons who were there but did not sign the
book. You will then provide each lobbyist with the names of
the guests and the total amount attributed to each guest for
attending the function.

Please call me at 2-6441 if you have further questions.

Sincerely,

Robert M. Stern
General Counsel

SACRAMENTO ADDRESS
STATE CAPITOL
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
TELEPHONE 916-445-4556

DISTRICT OFFICE
520 SO. SERRANO BLVD.
SUITE 408
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA
TELEPHONE
213-476-7646
213-382-2070
213-626-4762

Assembly

California Legislature

COMMITTEES:
BUSINESS AND
PROFESSIONS
ELECTIONS AND
REAPPORTIONMENT
JUDICIARY
WAYS AND MEANS
JOINT LEGISLATIVE
BUDGET COMMITTEE

HOWARD L. BERMAN
ASSEMBLYMAN

CHAIRMAN,
POLICY RESEARCH MANAGEMENT COMMITTEE

June 30, 1981

Robert Stern
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices
Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Bob:

     On Tuesday, July 7, 1981, the staff of my Capitol office has planned a going away party for Jack Mayesh who will be moving to Los Angeles. Some lobbyists have agreed to help us host this function which will be held at the Community Center from 5:30 p.m. to 8:30 p.m.

     For purposes of campaign reporting we will provide a guest registration book for those in attendance to sign. This will be at the entrance to the room we are using and will be "manned" from 5:30 p.m. to 6:30 p.m. After 6:30 p.m. a sign will be placed above the book advising guests who have not registered to do so. It is my understanding from a conversation you had on June 29, with Cleatta Simpson that lobbyists may then use the information in the guest registration book for purposes of reporting, and the fact that some guests may appear on more than one report for this event will not create a filing problem. Let me assure you that every effort will be made to register those in attendance.

     Does this meet with the filing requirements of the FPPC? I would appreciate a written response as soon as possible.

Sincerely,

HOWARD L. BERMAN

HLB:cs
